name: Cidacos and Préjano Greenways Loop
name_original: El Picuezo y la Picueza, las rocas de autol — desde Arnedo
description: Cycle 33.9 miles (54.5 km) from Arnedo to Autol, exploring the Cidacos Valley and the unique El Picuezo y la Picueza rock formations.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Arnedo are set within the picturesque Cidacos River valley in La Rioja, Spain. The region features diverse landscapes, including the gentle Vía Verde del Cidacos, a converted railway line, and the more challenging ascents of the Sierra de Cebollera. Cyclists can navigate along the tranquil Cidacos River, through traditional orchards, and into dense forests. The area offers varied terrain, from flat greenways to mountains reaching over 2,000 meters. Best road cycling routes around Arnedo The most popular road cycling route is Cidacos and Préjano Greenways Loop, a 33.9 miles (54.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 26 minutes to complete. This route follows the Vía Verde del Cidacos, offering a scenic ride along the river valley. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Arnedo and Préjano via Cidacos Greenway Loop, an easy 13.6 miles (21.9 km) path. This route provides a gentle ride through the riverine landscape, suitable for various cycling levels. Local road cyclists also love the Arnedillo and thermal baths – Autol and its Picuezos loop from Arnedo, a 68.3 miles (109.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 5 hours 28 minutes. Road cycling around Arnedo is defined by the Cidacos River valley, the Vía Verde del Cidacos, and the mountains of Sierra de Cebollera. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy greenways to challenging mountain climbs. The routes in Arnedo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Arnedo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Arnedo?
A: Arnedo offers a diverse selection of over 25 road cycling routes. These range from easy rides along river valleys to challenging ascents in the mountains, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of road cycling routes in Arnedo?
A: The routes around Arnedo vary significantly in difficulty. You'll find a couple of easy routes, around 10 moderate options, and approximately 15 more difficult trails, particularly those venturing into the Sierra de Cebollera.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Arnedo?
A: The region's climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for road cycling, offering pleasant temperatures. Summer can be warm, especially in the valley, while winter might present colder conditions, particularly at higher elevations in the Sierra de Cebollera.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, the Vía Verde del Cidacos offers gentle and scenic routes perfect for beginners or families. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383493552/arnedo-and-prejano-via-cidacos-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arnedo and Préjano via Cidacos Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 21.9 km path that provides a gentle ride through the riverine landscape.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, the Sierra de Cebollera region provides significant climbs and panoramic views, with mountains reaching over 2,000 meters. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31489489" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arnedillo and thermal baths – Autol and its Picuezos loop from Arnedo&lt;/a&gt;, at 109.9 km with over 1,000 meters of elevation gain, offer a demanding ride.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes in Arnedo?
A: Many routes in Arnedo are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990126769/cidacos-and-prejano-greenways-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos and Préjano Greenways Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the more extensive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40017663" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yanguas Historic Village – Arnedillo and thermal baths loop from Arnedo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions can I see or visit along the road cycling routes?
A: Along the routes, you can enjoy the tranquil Cidacos River Valley, glimpse the abrupt Peñas de Arnedillo, and explore the diverse landscapes of the Sierra de Cebollera Natural Reserve. For relaxation after a ride, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1751438" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozas de Aguas Termales de Arnedillo&lt;/a&gt; offer a soothing natural spa experience.

Q: Are there historical landmarks or cultural sites accessible by bike?
A: Yes, several historical sites are within cycling distance. You can visit the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5344965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Arnedo&lt;/a&gt; perched above the city, or explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5344963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Préjano Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The Monastery of Vico, with its rich history, is also accessible from the Vía Verde del Cidacos.

Q: What are the trail conditions like for road cycling in Arnedo?
A: The Vía Verde del Cidacos features a mixed surface of asphalt and compacted gravel, making it accessible for various cycling levels. Roads in the Sierra de Cebollera are generally well-maintained mountain roads, though conditions can vary with weather and elevation.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Arnedo?
A: The road cycling routes around Arnedo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 3 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from the gentle greenways to the challenging mountain climbs, and the scenic beauty of the Cidacos River valley.

Q: Are there routes that follow greenways or converted railway lines?
A: Yes, the Vía Verde del Cidacos is a prominent feature, offering a gentle and scenic route along a converted narrow-gauge railway line. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990126769/cidacos-and-prejano-greenways-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos and Préjano Greenways Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice that utilizes this greenway.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay near the cycling routes?
A: Arnedo and nearby towns like Arnedillo offer various amenities. Arnedillo, known for its thermal baths, has options for accommodation and places to eat, making it a good stop for refreshments or an overnight stay after a ride.
tips: It's as if someone had built mud castles on the beach with wet sand - that's what the washed-out rock formations in the town of Autol look like. Absolutely surreal and definitely worth a visit! Don't forget the photo!

Préjano is a town in the Rioja Baja. It is located on the left bank of the Ruesca River. It was an old mining town, remains that you can see when you pass by. Today its inhabitants support themselves thanks to agriculture and the industry of Arnedo, the closest town that has more life.

You can visit the two most representative churches in Préjano.

The city of Arnedo is part of Baja Rioja and is located at the foot of the Iberian System. It is a place where you can not only visit its churches, the monastery of Vico, do wine tourism or visit its cave of the hundred pillars, but also visit the area of shops and local shoe factories.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 54.5 kilometers (33.9 miles) long and typically takes around 2 hours and 26 minutes to complete by racebike. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your cycling speed and stops along the way.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features well-paved and generally smooth surfaces, making it ideal for road cycling. A significant portion follows the Cidacos Greenway, which is known for its excellent firm and mostly asphalted path with gentle inclines and descents. Be aware that some sections might be shared with vehicular traffic.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience the spectacular landscapes of the Cidacos Valley, featuring striking clay-colored cliffs, historic castles, and unique cliff houses. The route also passes through agricultural areas with olive and almond groves, vineyards, and lush riparian forests along the Cidacos River. The highlight is undoubtedly Autol and its Picuezos.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: While specific seasonal recommendations aren't provided, the moderate difficulty and varied terrain suggest that spring and autumn would offer pleasant temperatures for cycling. Summers might be hot, and winters could be chilly, so check the weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any unique geological features or local legends associated with El Picuezo y la Picueza?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The most significant features are El Picuezo and La Picueza in Autol. These are remarkable geological formations of sandstone and clay rock, sculpted by erosion to resemble a man (approx. 45m tall) and a woman (approx. 30m tall). A popular local legend tells of a couple turned to stone as punishment for lying about stealing grapes. They are located within a picturesque park adjacent to the Cidacos River, offering great photo opportunities.

faqs.question: Does this route follow any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, a significant portion of this route utilizes the former railway line, now transformed into the Cidacos Greenway. It also intersects with sections of the Camino Natural Via Verde del rio Cidacos, Vía Verde del Cidacos, and Vía Verde de Préjano.

faqs.question: Is the El Picuezo y la Picueza route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this is primarily a road cycling route, dogs are generally not recommended to accompany cyclists due to safety concerns with traffic and the pace of cycling. If you plan to visit the natural monument area of El Picuezo y la Picueza on foot, dogs are usually allowed on a leash in public park areas, but always check local regulations upon arrival.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this area?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required for cycling this route or visiting the natural monument of El Picuezo y la Picueza. The Cidacos Greenway is generally free to access. However, it's always a good idea to check for any local updates before your trip.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail, especially if starting from Arnedo?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Arnedo, being the starting point, is a town where you should be able to find public parking. Look for designated parking zones within Arnedo, especially near the beginning of the Cidacos Greenway or main access roads. Parking near the natural monument in Autol is also an option if you plan to start there.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as 'moderate' due to its distance of 54.5 km and an elevation gain of approximately 500 meters. While the terrain is mostly smooth, it requires a good level of physical fitness. Beginners to road cycling might find it challenging, but those with some experience and good stamina should be able to complete it.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a road cycling trip on this trail?
faqs.answer: For this moderate road cycling route, you should bring your racebike, a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, water bottles, snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), and navigation tools (like a GPS device or smartphone with the route loaded). Sunscreen and sunglasses are also recommended, especially during sunnier months.
